title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 17th February 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: One of the better auto-ancillary names, decent results
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said hold Talbros Auto, calling it one of the better auto-ancillary companies with decent results. It is near its 52-week high (recently ~300, stable ~280) with signs of crossing the 52-week highs again, helped by auto ancillaries doing well lately.

He said hold Senco Gold. The December result was eye-popping: revenue ~Rs 3,000 crore (up 100% QoQ) and net profit from Rs 53 crore to Rs 267 crore. With 320-330 as a major base, the stock can attempt 400 again, helped by speculator fancy and the precious-metals rally.

recommendations.note: Only for extreme long-term/contrarian; average if holding
recommendations.detail: He said buy Infosys only if you have a contrarian, extreme long-term view, as it is a positional trade. These IT majors (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Tech Mahindra) have huge cash reserves and will be compelled to reinvent for AI. For an existing holder needing cash he advised averaging Infosys rather than booking the loss.

recommendations.note: Sell to raise cash rather than book larger Infosys loss
recommendations.detail: For a caller needing money who held CDSL (~Rs 5,000 loss) and Infosys (~Rs 40,000 loss), Kutumba Rao said sell CDSL now, because booking the much larger Infosys loss is not right; he also suggested averaging Infosys. (Ramakrishna gave the opposite view, favouring selling Infosys.)

recommendations.note: Turnaround result, new high likely shortly
recommendations.detail: He said buy TVS Supply Chain on dips. After good turnaround results the stock already rose ~25-30% from around 95-96 and a new high looks likely very shortly. Since the recent rally was sharp, he would buy on a small correction toward 107-108 over the next 1-2 months.

recommendations.note: Start with only 25% of intended quantity
recommendations.detail: On 'Most' (Motilal Oswal), Kutumba Rao said buy with a medium-to-long-term view, but slowly — first buy only 25% of what you want. The recent quarter was flat; he advised watching the post-AI-summit narrative and any company announcements before adding more.

He said buy/accumulate NCC at current levels with a one-year-plus view, as the infra space appears to have bottomed out well and such stocks could perform in coming days. He cautioned it is not a quarterly-payout stock and is currently a bit weak on financials, so do not expect quick returns.

He said accumulate Coal India anywhere close to 400 or 410, medium-to-long term. PSU stocks act as a safety net with sound performance; subsidiary IPOs and the announced government disinvestment pattern are positives, and there is a good dividend payout. It rose ~3% the prior session.

recommendations.note: Hardware/server maker, not an IT software firm
recommendations.detail: On Network Technologies/Netweb he stressed it is a hardware company supplying servers and high-end computing solutions (and some software) to IT firms, so it should not be valued like software companies. It took a hit in the recent tech selloff (had gone to ~4,500, back to ~3,600), but results are good and there is no need to panic — hold.

recommendations.note: Attractive fundamentally; caveat is AI adaptation
recommendations.detail: He said hold Persistent Systems at current levels, since clarity still needs to come on whether the IT companies reinvent for AI. Fundamentally the stock looks attractive, but the caveat is whether it adapts to AI.

recommendations.note: Attractive fundamentally; caveat is AI adaptation
recommendations.detail: He said hold Coforge at current levels alongside Persistent, pending clarity on AI reinvention. Fundamentally it looks attractive, with the caveat being whether it adapts to AI.

He said if the holder wants to hold Sharath Industries, hold it. A former 'dark horse' (flagged at 40) on the aqua side that gave good returns earlier; recently dull with low volumes, around 170, close to its 52-week high. He cautioned it is more of a controlled/operated stock.

recommendations.note: Poor recent results, promoter pledging; wait and see
recommendations.detail: He advised waiting and watching rather than buying Tilaknagar Industries now. The recent results were poor (a loss despite a dramatic top-line jump and high capex), and promoters pledged maximum shares. He would wait to see whether a turnaround comes in the coming quarter before buying.

recommendations.note: Bought near 52-week low; large pharma name
recommendations.detail: He said hold Divi's Labs with nothing to fear. The caller bought near the 52-week low (~5,000; 52wk low 4,941) and the stock is already at ~6,100 (once went to 7,000). It is one of the better stocks to hold in an uncertain atmosphere, being one of the largest pharma market caps at Rs 1.6 lakh crore.
He said hold Suzlon at current levels. Due to institutional supply the stock has stagnated around 50 and needs a breakout from there before it can move higher.

He said hold IOB at current levels and exit on a rally. Government of India disinvestment (like the Bank of Maharashtra announcement) is expected, and it may be merged with another bank. When that announcement comes a small rally up to 42-43 could occur, which is the time to exit.

recommendations.note: Fell with IT; nothing wrong company-specific
recommendations.detail: On the caller's 50%-loss position, Kutumba Rao said it fell along with all IT stocks and there is nothing specifically wrong at the company. Recovery is likely only after IT sentiment improves (Ramakrishna added the chart is very weak with weak recent results).

recommendations.note: Monopoly PSU; correction is a buying opportunity
recommendations.detail: He said hold HAL, citing its monopoly status as a Government of India PSU counter. The current corrective mode is a buying opportunity, not a selling opportunity.
